content: The language learning definition of reflexion as the ability to reflect on one’s own writing to catch errors and refine expression can be directly borrowed to improve LLM self-reflection mechanisms, by framing LLM self-reflection as a second-language editing process rather than a purely logical error-checking task. Just as high school students learn to edit their own work by stepping back to critique their writing from a reader’s perspective, LLMs can be trained to use this same readerly reflexion habit to spot inconsistent or flawed outputs across multiple problem-solving perspectives.
